task: I’ve got a couple of notices and follow-up forms sitting here, along with a few calls I need to make. I’m trying to decide the best way to handle them so I can keep track of what’s been sent and what still needs attention.

turns.agent_response:
A simple way to keep this organized is to make one checklist for everything and track each item the same way.

### Use a 3-column list
| Item | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Notice/Form 1 | Not started / Sent / Called | Date mailed, where sent, any exact info used |
| Notice/Form 2 | Not started / Sent / Called | Same |
| Phone call 1 | Not made / Done | Date, who you spoke with, what they said |
| Phone call 2 | Not made / Done | Same |

### For each notice/form
- Compare the notice and follow-up form side by side.
- If they ask for the same fact, use the **same exact date/number** in both places.
- If there’s any difference, **flag it** so you can review it before sending.
- Keep a copy of the full packet for yourself.
- On your copy, write:
  - the **mailing date**
  - **where it was sent**
  - what was included

### For calls
- Make a separate call log with:
  - date and time
  - number called
  - person or department
  - summary of what was said
  - any follow-up needed

If you want, I can turn this into a **simple fill-in checklist** you can use right away for your notices, forms, and calls.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape. But I’d want it a little tighter, because ‘one checklist’ gets messy once the papers start piling up.

Make it more like this:
- one page for each notice/form packet
- one call log page
- a master list at the top with dates

What I need is a way to see, fast:
- what was mailed
- what was called about
- what’s still waiting
- what needs a callback or copy kept

Also put a line for:
- who has the original
- what exact date it was sent
- any reference number or case number

That’s the part that matters. If you want, give me the fill-in version with those fields built in.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter **fill-in version** with:

- **master list at the top**
- **one page per notice/form packet**
- **one call log page**
- fields for **mailed / called / waiting / callback / copy kept**
- a line for **original holder, sent date, and reference/case number**

---

# MASTER LIST
| Date | Item | Type | Sent / Called | Still Waiting? | Callback Needed? | Copy Kept? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ____ / ____ / ____ | __________________ | Notice/Form | Mailed / Sent | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No |
| ____ / ____ / ____ | __________________ | Notice/Form | Mailed / Sent | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No |
| ____ / ____ / ____ | __________________ | Call | Called | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No |
| ____ / ____ / ____ | __________________ | Call | Called | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No |

---

# NOTICE / FORM PACKET PAGE
**Packet name:** ______________________________________

**Reference / case number:** ____________________________

**Who has the original?** _______________________________

**Exact date sent / mailed:** ____________________________

**Sent to (name / office / address):** ___________________
_______________________________________________________

**What was mailed / sent:**  
_______________________________________________________  
_______________________________________________________

**What this packet is waiting on:**  
_______________________________________________________  
_______________________________________________________

**Callback needed?**  Yes / No  
If yes, by when: _______________________________________

**Copy kept?**  Yes / No  
**Where filed:** ________________________________________

**Notes / contradictions / missing items:**  
_______________________________________________________  
_______________________________________________________

---

# CALL LOG PAGE
| Date | Time | Number Called | Who / Dept. | Reference / Case # | What Was Said | Follow-Up Needed | Callback Date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ____ / ____ / ____ | ______ | ______________ | ______________ | ______________ | __________________ | Yes / No | __________ |
| ____ / ____ / ____ | ______ | ______________ | ______________ | ______________ | __________________ | Yes / No | __________ |
| ____ / ____ / ____ | ______ | ______________ | ______________ | ______________ | __________________ | Yes / No | __________ |

---

# QUICK CHECK FOR EACH PACKET
- **What was mailed?**
- **What was called about?**
- **What is still waiting?**
- **Is a callback needed?**
- **Was a copy kept?**
- **Who has the original?**
- **What exact date was it sent?**
- **What reference/case number goes with it?**

If you want, I can turn this into a **clean one-page printable form** with bigger writing space and less table clutter.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s usable.

The master list at the top is the part that keeps it from turning into a pile of loose paper. And the packet page has the fields that matter — original, sent date, case number, waiting on what, callback, copy kept. That’s enough to track it without playing detective later.

I’d probably print a few of the packet pages and keep the call log separate like you laid out. Cleaner that way.

If anything, the only thing I’d watch is leaving enough room for notes on the packet page. These notices always have some little inconsistency buried in them.
